chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH, KARACHI I. Appeals No.122 of 2023 I. Appeal No. 98 of 2024 Date Order with signature of the Judge 1. For orders on CMA 1236/2024. 2. For orders on office objections 3. For hearing of main case 4. For hearing of CMA 6000/23. 14.04.2025. Mr. Muhammad Azhar Afridi, advocate for appellant. Mr. Iftikhar Javed Kazi, advocate for respondent = MUHAMMAD IQBAL KALHORO J: Learned counsel for appellant by filing statements has pleaded for disposal of these appeals in the said terms. These statements contain an undertaking on behalf of appellant to pay to respondent or deposit decretal amount of Rs.12,460,000/- in 10 installments within a period of one year from today. This proposal has been accepted by learned counsel for respondent. In the light of such statement, it is ordered that appellant should pay the decretal amount as mentioned above to respondent in 10 equal installments within a period of one year from today. It is further informed that respondent is in possession of 09 cheques of different amounts to the tune of decretal amount issued by the appellant, which he may be directed to return to the appellant on payment of entire decretal amount. The respondent shall return all the 09 cheques to the appellant on payment of entire decretal amount as agreed. It has been further agreed by the parties that appellant would deposit each and every installment before Nazir of this court, who will disburse the same to the respondent on his proper verification and identification. On payment of entire decretal amount in the manner as above, the respondent shall handover all the 09 cheques as mentioned above to the Nazir, who on having been satisfied that decretal amount has been realized, would hand over the same to the appellant in accordance with law.

It has been further agreed by the parties that appellant would deposit each and every installment before Nazir of this court, who will disburse the same to the respondent on his proper verification and identification. On payment of entire decretal amount in the manner as above, the respondent shall handover all the 09 cheques as mentioned above to the Nazir, who on having been satisfied that decretal amount has been realized, would hand over the same to the appellant in accordance with law. The order already passed in Execution Application being challenged in connected Ist Appeal No.98/2024 shall remain intact but stayed till satisfaction of the decree and after such satisfaction, would automatically merge in this order. The Ist Appeals are disposed of in the above terms alongwith pending applications. JUDGE JUDGE A.K
